# The Westinghouse Time Capsule

## Overview

The Westinghouse Time Capsule was buried at the 1939 World's Fair to be recovered five thousand years later, holding a curated greeting and cross-section of contemporary culture.

## Who chose what it carried

The capsule was overseen by a small committee of engineers who spent months arguing over what a curated portrait of an entire civilization should contain. They selected and arranged everything that went into it: written greetings in many human languages, the recorded sounds and newsreels of the day, an archive of music drawn from many cultures and decades, and a long sequence of photographs depicting people, landscapes, and the chemistry of life. Every inclusion was debated, because the committee knew the capsule might be the only trace of them that ever survived.
